title: Buttery Dense Middle Eastern Lentils Mujadara
description: A rich and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring lentils, rice, and crispy onions, perfect for a date night dinner. It is served with warm pita bread and a side of tangy tadka.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and rice, then soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are crispy and golden brown.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the saucepan and stir for 1 minute.
Add the lentils and rice to the saucepan, stirring to combine with the onion mixture.
Add 4 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the lentils and rice are tender.
Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter and stir into the lentil mixture.
Serve the mujadara hot, garnished with crispy onions and accompanied by warm pita bread.
